Monash University · Faculty of Engineering · Final Year Project 2024

Semi-Autonomous Caramel Wrapping

Automating boutique confectionery packaging for 338 Degrees · Project ID: ME-21-OKEV-008
Aakaash Singh Chauhan · Hai-Ning Cheng · Paul Baskaraboopathi
Kevin Otto · Jonathan Eden
338 Degrees Confectionery
<5sTarget cycle time
5Simultaneous units
±1mmFold tolerance
2Caramel size variants
150–200°CSeal temperature
★
System CAD — Assembly Overview
1
Background & Problem Statement

338 Degrees currently wraps caramels individually using a manual impulse heat sealer, resulting in a cycle time of ~25 seconds per unit. This limits scalability for small-batch manufacturing and introduces operator-dependent quality variation in a premium product line.

Benchtop / portable form factor
Artisanal hand-crafted aesthetic
Standard & half-length caramel support
Biodegradable wrap compatibility
Small-batch throughput targets
Minimal manual intervention per cycle
2
Engineering Requirements
RequirementTarget
Wrapping Cycle Time<5 sec/unit
Fold Symmetry±1mm tolerance
Size Variants2 (Standard / Half-length)
Sealing Temperature150°C – 200°C
Batch Size5 units simultaneous
Form FactorBenchtop / portable
3
Concept Generation — Seal Design Downselection

Six sealing and wrapping configurations were developed and evaluated against criteria of manufacturability, aesthetic quality, geometric precision, dual-size compatibility, and seal complexity.

2-Sided Seal: D1 ✓ SELECTED
Ease of mfg · Aesthetic · Precision · Dual-size compatible
2-Sided Seal: D2 Rejected
Manufacturing complexity
3-Sided Seal: D1 Rejected
Complex seal design
4-Sided Seal Rejected
Unfavourable client feedback
2-Sided Seal: D3 Rejected
Iterative refinement — higher complexity
3-Sided Seal: D2 Rejected
Complex seal design

✓ 2-Sided Seal — Selected

  • Ease of manufacturing
  • Premium aesthetic quality
  • Geometric precision achieved
  • Compatible with both caramel sizes
  • Lowest seal design complexity

4
Sealing Phase — Thermal Control & Wire Selection

Precise thermal management was required to seal biodegradable packaging without damaging adjacent components. Two sealing technologies were evaluated.

🔌
Impulse Heat Sealer
Add photo here · 30s @ 195°C — backup redundancy
Impulse Heat Sealer (Backup)
⚡
NiCr Wire Design ✓ Selected
Add NiCr wire photo here · 0.66s @ 7A optimised
NiCr 80/20 Wire — Selected
FEA: Heat localised to seal interface
NiCr Wire — Energy & Time Calculation
Material:NiCr 80/20 wire
Energy required:14.38 J
Initial (1.2A):Time = 14.38 / (1.2² × 0.444) = 22.5 sec
Optimised (7A):Time = 14.38 / (7² × 0.444) = 0.66 sec
FEA confirmed operating temperatures remain localised (150°C–200°C) at the sealing interface, preventing thermal damage to adjacent machine components.
✓ 0.66 sec seal time achieved
5
Batch Processing & Positioning Jig

A specialised positioning jig processes 5 caramels simultaneously, ensuring high-precision alignment during the packaging cycle. Consistent geometry within ±1mm tolerance is guaranteed across all units, eliminating manual placement error.

5 units loaded simultaneously
Calibrated slot positions
±1mm geometric tolerance
Dual-size compatible slots
Loading sequence — 4 steps
📷
Physical Jig Photo
Add photo of actual jig here
★
Final Prototype

2-sided seal mechanism with custom positioning jig. Structural design and control system validated. Heating wire excluded from final demonstration due to safety requirements.

5-slot positioning jig — precision batch alignment
Slot-guided fold — ±1mm symmetry
2-sided thermal seal — validated
Dual-size caramel support
📷

7
Discussion & Future Work

Key Achievements

  • Custom positioning jig achieves consistent ±1mm fold symmetry
  • System replicates hand-wrapped aesthetics for dual-size variants
  • Validated food-safe design for small-scale confectionery use

Future Work & Scalability

  • Thermal sealing module integration — pending certified professional validation
  • Replace manual loading with automated feeding to achieve <5s target
  • Transition provides a scalable production roadmap for 338 Degrees
